title: STANDARD FOR EDIBLE CASSAVA FLOUR CXS 176-1989

sections.section: STANDARD FOR EDIBLE CASSAVA FLOUR CXS 176-1989
sections.summary: Title/header only for the Codex 'Standard for Edible Cassava Flour (CXS 176‑1989)' with no substantive provisions in this section.

sections.section: Adopted in 1989. Revised in 1995. Amended in 2013, 2019, 2023.
sections.summary: Provides the standard's adoption and revision history (adopted 1989; revised 1995; amended 2013, 2019, 2023) and notes that amendments were made to the Annex following the 2023 Commission decisions.
Defines the scope: applies to cassava flour intended for direct human consumption produced from edible cassava (Manihot esculenta Crantz).

Describes the product definition section, stating edible cassava flour is made from dried cassava chips or paste by pounding/grinding/milling and sifting, and that bitter cassava requires detoxification by soaking before drying.

Defines edible cassava flour as the product from dried cassava chips or paste processed by pounding, grinding or milling and sifting to remove fibre, and specifies detoxification by soaking for bitter cassava prior to drying.

Sets general quality requirements for edible cassava flour: it must be safe and suitable for human consumption, free from abnormal flavours/odours and living insects, and free from filth of animal origin in amounts that could hazard health.

sections.section: 3.1.1 Edible cassava flour shall be safe and suitable for human consumption.
sections.summary: Requires edible cassava flour to be safe and suitable for human consumption.

sections.section: 3.1.2 Edible cassava flour shall be free from abnormal flavours, odours, and living insects.
sections.summary: Requires edible cassava flour to be free from abnormal flavours, odours and living insects.

sections.section: 3.1.3 Edible cassava flour shall be free from filth (impurities of animal origin, including dead insects) in amounts which may represent a hazard to human health.
sections.summary: Requires edible cassava flour to be free from filth (impurities of animal origin, including dead insects) in amounts that may present a hazard to human health.
Heading only for '3.2 Quality factors - specific' with no substantive provisions in this section text.

Specifies a maximum moisture content of 13% m/m for edible cassava flour and notes that lower moisture limits may be required for certain destinations depending on climate, transport duration and storage.
States that the product must comply with maximum levels in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193‑1995) and with Codex pesticide maximum residue limits.
Requires the product to comply with the maximum contaminant and toxin levels set out in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193‑1995).
Requires the product to comply with pesticide maximum residue limits established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

Recommends preparing and handling the product according to the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1‑1969) and relevant Codes of Practice, requires the product to be free from objectionable matter, and when tested to be free from microorganisms, parasites or microbial substances that may represent a health hazard.

Requires cassava flour packaging to safeguard hygienic, nutritional, technological and organoleptic qualities, use safe and suitable packaging materials that do not impart toxic substances or undesirable odours/flavours, and where sacks are used they must be clean, sturdy and securely closed.

sections.section: 6.1 Cassava flour shall be packaged in containers which will safeguard the hygienic, nutritional, technological, and organoleptic qualities of the product.
sections.summary: Requires that containers used for cassava flour protect its hygienic, nutritional, technological and organoleptic qualities.

sections.section: 6.2 The containers, including packaging material, shall be made of substances which are safe and suitable for their  intended  use.  They  should  not  impart  any  toxic  substance  or  undesirable  odour  or  flavour  to  the product.
sections.summary: Requires packaging materials and containers to be safe and suitable for intended use and not to impart toxic substances or undesirable odour or flavour to the cassava flour.

sections.section: 6.3 When the product is packaged in sacks, these must be clean, sturdy and strongly sewn or sealed.
sections.summary: Specifies that when cassava flour is packaged in sacks, the sacks must be clean, sturdy and strongly sewn or sealed.

Specifies that, in addition to the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods (CXS 1‑1985), additional, product‑specific labelling provisions apply to this standard (edible cassava flour).
Requires the product name on the label to be exactly 'edible cassava flour'.

For non‑retail containers, information may be on the container or accompanying documents, except the product name, lot identification and the manufacturer/packer name and address must appear on the container, although lot identification and name/address may be replaced by an identification mark if that mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.
States that compliance checking shall use the methods of analysis and sampling in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234‑1999) relevant to this standard.
